sentences.base_language: I get up and wash my face.
sentences.grammarNote:
**起床** (qǐchuáng) = get out of bed. **起** (rise) + **床** (bed). A verb-object compound used as a unit: **起床了** (got up), **该起床了** (time to get up).
**洗脸** (xǐ liǎn) = wash (one's) face. Serial verbs: two actions with the same subject listed one after another — **起床洗脸** (get up and wash face). No conjunction needed.
**脸** (liǎn) = face. Contains the **月** (flesh) radical.

sentences.base_language: I brush my teeth for two minutes.
sentences.grammarNote:
When a verb-object compound (like **刷牙**) has a duration, the verb is repeated: **刷牙刷两分钟** (brush teeth for two minutes). The pattern is: V+O + V + duration. This is because **了**/duration attaches to a verb, not the compound.
Same pattern with other hygiene verbs: **跑步跑了三十分钟** (ran for thirty minutes), **洗澡洗了二十分钟** (showered for twenty minutes).
**两** (liǎng) = two (used before measure words: **两分钟**, **两个人**). **二** (èr) = two (used in numbers and counting: **第二** second).

**梳** (shū) = to comb/brush (hair). **梳头** or **梳头发** both mean 'comb one's hair' — the shorter **梳头** is also common.
**头发** (tóufa) = head hair. **头** (head) + **发** (grow/hair). The **发** in **头发** typically has a neutral tone (tóufa), softened from its stand-alone 4th tone (**fà**).
For hair care: **梳头** (comb), **洗头** (wash hair), **剪头发** (cut hair), **染头发** (dye hair).

sentences.base_language: My hair is long and black.
sentences.romanized: Wǒ de tóufa yòu cháng yòu hēi.
sentences.grammarNote:
**又…又…** (yòu…yòu…) = 'both … and …'. It connects two adjectives: **又长又黑** (both long and black), **又聪明又漂亮** (both smart and beautiful). Always two qualities, both applied to the same subject.
Note: **又** alone means 'again' — it's only the **又…又…** double pattern that means 'both…and'. **又长又黑** ≠ 'again long again black'.

**穿上** = 'put on (clothes)'. **上** (shàng) as a directional complement means the action results in something being on: **穿上** (put on), **戴上** (put on accessories/hats). The opposite is **脱下** (take off clothing).
**穿** (chuān) = to wear/put on (clothing that you step into or through). **戴** (dài) = to wear (accessories, glasses, hats — things you place on). So: **穿衣服** (wear clothes) but **戴眼镜** (wear glasses).

**照镜子** = look in the mirror. **照** (zhào) = to illuminate/reflect/photograph. Used in: **照镜子** (look in mirror), **照相** (take photos), **照片** (photograph).
**镜子** (jìngzi) = mirror. **镜** = mirror/lens; **子** = nominalizing suffix. Also: **眼镜** (glasses — 'eye mirror'), **望远镜** (telescope — 'look-far mirror'), **显微镜** (microscope — 'show-tiny mirror').

**准备好了** = 'have gotten ready' (**准备** + **好** resultative complement + **了** change of state). **好** as a result complement means 'successfully done / properly done': **写好了** (finished writing), **做好了** (done/ready).
**终于** (zhōngyú) = finally — expresses relief after a long wait. Compare **最终** (zuìzhōng, ultimately/in the end — more neutral). **终于** always carries an emotional sense of 'at last!'.

**出门** = 'go out the door / leave home'. The sentence-final **了** signals a change of state: 'I (have now) left the house.' This use of **了** is very common to announce that something just happened or a new situation has begun.
**出** (chū) = exit/go out. Many directional compound verbs: **出门** (leave home), **出院** (leave hospital), **出发** (set out/depart). The opposite **进门** means 'enter the house'.
